_instructions: This file draws the 'Upper Logan Canyon' schematic page (Franklin Basin down to the UWRL Water Lab).
See public/schematics/README.md for the full field reference and a longer walkthrough - this is the quick version.
NODES: each node is a card on the diagram.
id   - unique within this file, used by edges below to connect nodes. Never change an existing id casually - edges reference it.

name - for type 'station', must fuzzy-match a live station's display name from HydroServer/DWRi/USGS (partial matches work, e.g. 'Beaver Creek' will match 'Beaver Creek: Above Diversion'). For 'junction'/'terminus'/'extension' nodes it is just label text.
row  - vertical position, smaller = further upstream/higher on the page.
col  - horizontal lane. In this file: 2 = main channel, 1 = left-bank tributaries/canals, 3 = right-bank tributaries/canals.

type - 'station' (shows live data), 'junction' (an invisible connection point on the main channel, no card), 'terminus' (a labeled endpoint banner), or 'extension' (a card that links to a different schematic page when clicked).
colorGroup - optional; must match a name from waterway_groups or schematic_groups in config/station_config.yaml, used to tint the card's background.
EDGES: each edge draws a connector line from one node id to another.
style - 'main' (thick dark line along the main channel), 'branch' (thinner line for a tributary or diversion), 'thin' (light gray line, used for secondary/independent flows).
direction - 'in' (water flowing into the main channel, e.g. a tributary) or 'out' (water flowing away, e.g. a diversion/canal). Defaults to 'in'.
To add a new station: add a node with type 'station', then add an edge connecting it to whichever junction (or station) it flows into.
To add a new diversion/canal: same as above, but set edge direction to 'out'.
This page continues into 'Lower Logan River' via the 'ext_to_lower' extension node at the bottom - do not remove it without also updating lower-logan.json's matching recap section.
Canals/diversions (DWRi stations) are intentionally left off this page for now - only natural tributaries (Beaver Creek, Ricks Spring, Temple Fork, Right Hand Fork, Dewitt Springs) are shown.
title: Upper Logan River: Canyon
subtitle: Franklin Basin down to the UWRL Water Lab
